trivia: The core of Boeing's fourth 737 generation pairs the classic airframe with LEAP-1B engines and split-tip winglets. After a grounding that made world news, it returned to become one of the most common jets in the sky again.
The double-pointed AT winglet, one blade up and one down, is the fastest way to tell a MAX from an NG at distance.
funFacts: The distinctive chevron-edged engine nacelles cut noise.
Thousands are on order, so this tile only gets easier.
